seoTitle: How to Connect an OData Feed to Power BI Step by Step
metaDescription: Follow this step-by-step guide to connect an OData feed to Power BI, pulling in real-time data from finance, marketing, or logistics sources.
intro: OData feeds are a common way systems expose structured, queryable data, and connecting one to Power BI opens up real-time reporting possibilities. This guide walks through setting up that connection from start to finish. It's built for analysts and business users who want to pull live data from an OData source into their dashboards.

steps.heading: Understand your OData feed
steps.body: Before connecting, know the feed's URL and whether it requires authentication, since this determines how you'll configure the connection in Power BI.

steps.heading: Connect the OData feed to Power BI
steps.body: Use Get Data in Power BI Desktop, select the OData Feed connector, and paste in the feed URL to begin the connection process.

steps.heading: Configure your data source settings
steps.body: Choose the authentication method required by the feed, whether that's anonymous access, a key, or organizational credentials, and select which tables to load.

steps.heading: Troubleshoot common setup issues
steps.body: If the connection fails, double-check the URL for typos, confirm the feed is publicly accessible or that your credentials are correct, and verify firewall settings.

steps.heading: Automate data refreshes
steps.body: Once connected, set up a scheduled refresh so the report continues pulling updated data from the OData feed on a regular basis.

faq.question: What is an OData feed, in simple terms?
faq.answer: It's a standardized way for a system to expose its data over the web so other applications, like Power BI, can query and read it directly.

faq.question: Do I need special permissions to connect to an OData feed?
faq.answer: That depends on the source system; some feeds are open, while others require an API key or organizational login credentials to access.

faq.question: Why is my OData connection timing out?
faq.answer: This can happen with very large feeds or slow source systems, so it's worth narrowing the data being requested or checking with the feed provider about performance.

faq.question: Can I filter the data before it loads into Power BI?
faq.answer: Yes, Power Query lets you apply filters after connecting, and some OData feeds also support query parameters to limit data at the source.
